heading: What does this mean for Google Cloud or Azure (Microsoft Azure) clusters in peering-only mode?
body:
Before 31 March 2020, you were required to enable peering-only mode to
connect to databases on peer networked Azure (Microsoft Azure) or Google Cloud clusters. This
mode:

- Affected global DNS (Domain Name System) resolution and

- Limited any database connections outside the peered network.

Multiple horizons lifts these restrictions and unlocks the following
additional features:

- [Charts](charts)

- Live Migration

To leverage multiple horizons, complete the following tasks:

- Update your application's existing connection strings to use
  [Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).

- [Disable Peering-Only mode](disable-peering-mode).

- Connect using the strings outlined in [Why does my cluster have multiple connection strings](atlas-faq-multi-conn-strings).

> **Note:** You *can* keep connecting to your clusters using existing
> peering-enabled connection strings at this time. Peering-Only mode
> prevents access to the improved functionality and reduced
> limitations of multiple horizons. To use the new features and remove
> the legacy limitations, MongoDB requires that you use the new
> connection strings.
